title: Announcement to companies approved by the CCAF
text:
In the perspective of the updating of their statutes by Monegasque public limited companies in accordance with Law No. 1.573 of April 8, 2025, concerning the modernization of company law, the CCAF (Commission de Controle des Activités Financières) reminds approved companies that, pursuant to the provisions of Article 8 of Law No. 1.338 of September 7, 2007, on financial activities, any modification of one or more key elements of the approval file requires the Commission’s prior authorization.

Therefore, modifications concerning, but not limited to, the share capital, corporate purpose, as well as management and administration of the company must be submitted to the CCAF for prior approval.

Furthermore, prior authorization from the Commission is also required when modifications relating to the management and administration procedures result in the creation of new positions (for example, Chairman of the Board, Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director) that are held by individuals for whom CCAF authorization has already been granted.

The CCAF therefore invites the companies concerned to contact the General Secretariat in order to ensure compliance with the provisions of Law No. 1.338 of September 7, 2007 mentioned above.

title: Warning concerning “FORBES PRIVATE BANK MONACO SA”
text:
The Commission de Contrôle des Activités Financières (“CCAF”) has become aware of an entity operating under the name of “FORBES PRIVATE BANK MONACO SA” (“the Entity”) which has an internet presence at https://forbesprivatebank.com. The Entity claims to be offering banking services in Monaco.

The CCAF wishes to alert the public that “FORBES PRIVATE BANK MONACO SA” is NEITHER a Monacan registered Company NOR licensed by the CCAF to provide any financial services which are required to be licensed under Monaco law. The Entity is not authorised to offer its services/ products to private customers in the Principality of Monaco.

The CCAF would like to remind consumers of financial services not to enter into any financial services transaction unless they have ascertained that the entity with whom the transaction is being made is authorised to provide such services by the CCAF.

A list of entities licensed by the CCAF can be viewed on our office website: List of authorised firms

title: Signing of a cooperation agreement between the CCAF and the AMSF
text:
The Commission de Contrôle des Activités Financières (Financial Activities Supervisory Commission) and the Autorité Monégasque de Sécurité Financière (Monegasque Financial Security Authority) signed an agreement in Monaco on February 13, 2026, aimed at organizing and strengthening their cooperation.

This agreement formalizes a procedure for mutual assistance and consultation to facilitate the performance of their respective missions and to consolidate Monaco’s framework for combating money laundering, terrorist financing, the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, and corruption.

It notably provides for:

a structured and secure exchange of information;

mutual assistance in strict compliance with professional secrecy;

enhanced coordination of control programs and risk-based approach methodologies; and

regular consultations on matters of common interest.

Through this agreement, the two authorities reaffirm their shared commitment to an ethical, transparent financial framework that complies with international standards.

A further step in strengthening Monaco’s financial security.

title: The CCAF participated in the 23rd session of IFREFI
text: The CCAF participated in the 23rd session of the French-speaking Institute of Financial Regulation (IFREFI), which was held in Rabat, Morocco, from June 30 to July 2, 2025. This event brought together representatives of financial regulatory authorities from nearly 30 member countries in the French-speaking world. The challenges and role of capital markets in infrastructure financing were at the heart of the discussions at this session.

title: Warning concerning “GlobalCIM”
text:
The Commission de Contrôle des Activités Financières (“CCAF”) has become aware of an entity operating under the name of “GlobalCIM” (“the Entity”) which has an internet presence at www.globalcim.com. The Entity claims to be offering “trading platforms” in Monaco.

The CCAF wishes to alert the public that “GlobalCIM” is NEITHER a Monacan registered Company NOR licensed by the CCAF to provide any financial services which are required to be licensed under Monaco law. The Entity is not authorised to offer its services/ products to private customers in the Principality of Monaco.

The CCAF would like to remind consumers of financial services not to enter into any financial services transaction unless they have ascertained that the entity with whom the transaction is being made is authorised to provide such services by the CCAF.

A list of entities licensed by the CCAF can be viewed on our office website: List of authorised firms

title: New members appointed
text:
The Commission de Contrôle des Activités Financières (“CCAF”) has two new members

Florence ROUSSEL, who replace Bruno GIZARD,

Christian DURAND.

For further informations, see the page members presentation

The CCAF would like to thanks Bruno GIZARD for his contribution to it during seven-teen year.
